IGUI_PD_PauseGrowthTip: Dogs that never grow up? Wish real life had that toggle :D
IGUI_PD_NeedFood: You need a food item in your inventory.
IGUI_PD_NeedWater: You need a water container in your inventory.
IGUI_PD_Released: You released %1 back to the wild.
IGUI_PD_NotYourDog: This %1 already belongs to another survivor.
IGUI_PD_DrivingDisabledTip: Not available while driving, your companion is riding with you.
IGUI_PD_Sick: The %1 gets sick from the bad food.
IGUI_PD_BadFood: Bad for dogs - eating this makes it sick and breaks its trust.
IGUI_PD_WoundedCritical: Your %1 is badly wounded - it will only follow until it heals.
IGUI_PD_PanicWarn: Your %1 is panicking - it won't fight.
IGUI_PD_PetCooldown: Your %1 has had enough affection for now.
IGUI_PD_Moodle_BreedHappy_desc: The Caramelo is a wonderfully affectionate, charismatic mutt - its loyal company lifts your spirits, so sadness and boredom fade faster while it's near.
IGUI_PD_Refused_disloyal: %1 doesn't trust you enough to obey.
IGUI_PD_Refused_wounded: %1 is too badly hurt to fight.
IGUI_PD_NoTarget: Your %1 finds nothing it can reach to attack.
IGUI_PD_ResetSpawnsTip: Clears the saved per-building spawn decisions so stray dogs can appear again across the map. Each building re-rolls on its next chunk load - for areas already loaded, move away and back (or relog).
IGUI_PD_SkillDesc_scent: The %1's nose. A higher Scent widens its sentinel range, sensing zombies from farther away.
IGUI_PD_SkillDesc_combat: Fighting prowess. Raises the damage it deals and toughens it against bites while it fights.
IGUI_PD_SkillDesc_obedience: Discipline and training. Keeps the %1 reliable at lower loyalty, lowering its disobey threshold.
IGUI_PD_LoyaltyDesc: How much the %1 trusts you. It slowly fades over time - faster while it's hungry or thirsty - and is restored by feeding, giving water and petting. If it drops too low the %1 turns disobedient, refusing to follow or fight until you win it back. The Obedience skill keeps it reliable at lower loyalty.
IGUI_PD_NeedDesc_hunger: Rises over time; feeding lowers it. Hunger throttles healing - a fed %1 recovers wounds fastest, the recovery slows as hunger climbs and stops once it passes the halfway mark. Very high hunger also drains the %1's health directly.
IGUI_PD_NeedDesc_thirst: Rises over time; giving water lowers it. Like hunger, thirst throttles natural healing - the thirstier the %1, the slower it recovers, stopping past the halfway mark. Severe thirst also drains its health directly.
IGUI_PD_NeedDesc_health: The %1's life. Fighting no longer wounds it (that only stresses it) - health falls only from real neglect, as severe hunger or thirst drains it, and at zero it dies for good. It heals slowly while fed, watered and out of combat.
IGUI_PD_NeedDesc_stress: Builds from hunger, thirst, low loyalty and FIGHTING - every strike agitates the %1, more when it's swarmed. A stressed %1 barks on its own, drawing zombies; if it peaks into panic it won't fight, guard or keep watch - though it still flees to you when you call it. Combat stress winds down on its own out of a fight, and petting, feeding or watering calms it faster. The Stress gene sets how easily it frays.
IGUI_PD_GenomeDesc: Inborn genes, fixed for life - rolled when the %1 is tamed and varying from one to the next. Unlike skills they never change with training; they are the raw nature this %1 was born with.
IGUI_PD_GeneDesc_strength: Raw muscle. Higher Strength makes the %1 hit a little harder in combat.
IGUI_PD_GeneDesc_aggressiveness: Fighting drive. A more aggressive %1 earns combat XP faster, so it learns to fight quicker. It does not change when the %1 decides to engage or retreat.
IGUI_PD_GeneDesc_resistance: Toughness. Higher Resistance keeps the %1 calmer under fire, so it gains less stress while fighting.
IGUI_PD_CmdFollowDesc: The %1 paths along behind you and fights zombies that get close. Its sentinel keeps watch. The default - your companion on the move.
IGUI_PD_CmdStayDesc: The %1 holds its position and won't follow. It still senses and warns about zombies, but it won't chase or fight - it just sits and watches. Good for parking it somewhere safe.
IGUI_PD_CmdGuardDesc: The %1 anchors to this exact spot and defends it - engaging zombies in a wider radius, alerting, then returning to its post after a chase. It backs off behind you if surrounded. (For now it only works while you are nearby.)
IGUI_PD_AlertFullDesc: Full watch over the whole sentinel range. A quiet growl for distant or few zombies; a loud bark for close, many or visible ones - and that bark draws zombies (the trade-off). The default.
IGUI_PD_AlertQuietDesc: Stealth watch: only a small nearby ring, and never more than a quiet growl and a halo - no loud bark, no noise that draws zombies. A discreet heads-up that won't give away your position.
IGUI_PD_AlertSilentDesc: No zombie alerts at all from this %1's sentinel. (Its stress barking from hunger or injury is separate and still happens.)
IGUI_PD_AutoProtectDesc: Your %1 stands guard while you do something that leaves you defenseless - sleeping, eating, reading, sitting or fishing. It anchors next to you and fights zombies off, then goes back to following the moment you're done. Fighting like this stresses the dog (it barks and draws zombies) and earns no loyalty, so use it wisely. Off by default; toggle it per dog.
IGUI_PD_BreedDesc_caramelo: The caramelo is a basic, everyday breed - the mongrel you find all over the cities. It has no special skills and is weak by nature: it can't kill a zombie on its own, only knock one down to buy you time. The plain baseline that future breeds will improve on.
IGUI_PD_CmdFetchDesc: Whistle your %1 to teleport instantly to your side - for when it is stuck or loaded but too far to walk back. The loud whistle draws zombies, the cost of an instant recall.
IGUI_PD_BtnRenameDesc: Give your companion a custom name (up to 30 characters). It sticks through save and reload.
IGUI_PD_BtnReleaseDesc: Send your companion back to the wild. It keeps its training and skills, so you can tame it again later.
IGUI_PD_BreedDesc_germanshepherd: A powerful, intelligent working dog. Born far stronger and tougher than the caramelo, it learns Combat and Obedience quickly, barely frays in a fight (a third of the stress), holds the line against a swarm without panicking, and can bring a zombie down on its own. Rare - found around police and military buildings.
IGUI_PD_BreedDesc_retriever: A friendly, keen-nosed gun dog and a natural hunter: it learns Hunting twice as fast and Combat a little faster than the caramelo, and can bring a zombie down on its own (though it frays more easily than the German Shepherd). Its nose helps you forage and track game from farther away. Uncommon - turns up now and then in residential houses.
IGUI_PD_BreedDesc_husky: A hardy northern sled dog: by far the strongest breed, built to haul cargo (its saddlebag carries the most) and to survive the cold. It learns Combat a little faster than the caramelo and can bring a zombie down, but it is no warrior. Stubborn (slower to learn Obedience), friendly (a poor guard) and a big eater (gets hungry fast). Rare - found only in the cold of winter, and at pet shops and vets.
IGUI_PD_Moodle_GSCourage_desc: A bold German Shepherd at your side steadies your nerves - its fearless presence eases your panic while it stays close.
IGUI_PD_Moodle_GoldenNose_desc: Your Golden Retriever works the ground with you. While it stays close, fed and loyal, you spot forage from farther away and learn Foraging, Trapping and Aiming faster.
IGUI_PD_Moodle_Warmed_desc: Your husky pulls its weight at your side. While it stays close, fed and loyal, it shoulders a little of your load so you can carry more and helps you keep your wind, so you tire more slowly; and when the cold sets in, its thick coat keeps you warm.
IGUI_PD_Moodle_Sick_desc: You fed it something dogs shouldn't eat - your dog is intoxicated and feeling ill.
IGUI_PD_Moodle_Hunger_desc: Your dog is hungry. Feed it before its health starts to suffer.
IGUI_PD_Moodle_Thirst_desc: Your dog is thirsty. Give it water before its health starts to suffer.
IGUI_PD_Moodle_Rested_desc: You slept soundly with your dog watching over you. For a while you feel calm and rested - panic, stress, sadness and boredom fade faster, and you tire more slowly.
IGUI_PD_DishNoSpace: No room to place the dish here.
IGUI_PD_DishFull: The bowl doesn't have room for all of that.
IGUI_PD_DishGone: The dish is no longer there.
IGUI_PD_DishNoWater: You need a water container to fill it.
IGUI_PD_BowlNeedFood: You have no dog food to add.
IGUI_PD_BowlHasWater: The bowl has water - empty it to add food.
IGUI_PD_BowlHasFood: The bowl has food - empty it to add water.
IGUI_PD_SkillDesc_hunt: The dog's hunting instinct. It levels up by pointing game and foraging. L0: points game and sniffs out frogs/bugs. L3: takes small game (rabbit, raccoon). L6: takes on deer and brings the kill back.
IGUI_PD_HuntModeDesc: When on, your %1 tracks wild game while following you and - once trained enough - chases it down: killing small game for you and wearing large game like deer down for you to finish. Hunting is a rural activity (you find game in forests and the countryside, not in town). The cost is symmetric: the dog leaves your side and is exposed, the chase barks and can draw zombies, large game can hurt it, it can wander off, and hunting works up an appetite. Off by default; toggle it per dog.
IGUI_PD_KillsDesc: Zombies and wild game this dog has killed. Turn on the sandbox option to also add zombie kills to your own count.
IGUI_PD_HuntDelivered: Your %1 dragged its kill to you.
IGUI_PD_HuntWoundedDeer: Your %1 wounded a deer - finish it off!
IGUI_PD_HuntTrackWounded: Your %1 is tracking the wounded game...
IGUI_PD_ForageFound: Your %1 found something! Turn on Search Mode to see it.
IGUI_PD_BagMapNoAnnot: Map markings aren't kept in the saddlebag.
IGUI_PD_KennelTooltip: All your bonded dogs - even the ones far away.
IGUI_PD_KennelStatusNoData: No record yet, visit this dog once
IGUI_PD_KennelRecallConfirm: Call %1 back from afar? Your loud whistle will draw zombies.
IGUI_PD_Refused_invehicle: %1 is riding in your vehicle, it comes back when you get out.
IGUI_PD_Refused_carried: %1 is in your arms, just put it down.
IGUI_PD_Refused_nodata: There is no record of that dog to call it back.
IGUI_PD_DogLost: %1 has gone missing! Open My Dogs (paw button) to call it back.
IGUI_PD_Refused_recallcd: %1 was called recently, wait a while before calling again.
